message: Contract review silently drops malformed structured finding output and can report no findings even when the reviewer attempted to emit findings.
evidence: ContractReview discards parseReviewerFindingBlocks warnings and invalid finding objects; the shared parser documents that a schema marker with no parsed block is a parse miss, not a clean review.

message: Human next-step output after accepting a contract draft still points directly to `pactum contract approve` instead of the configured contract review command, so the required pre-approval contract review affordance is skipped on this path.
evidence: `ContractAcceptDraft` computes `Next` with `nextCommandsForRun`, but `writeContractAcceptDraft` ignores it and hard-codes `pactum contract approve %s`; configured `contract.reviewers` therefore are not surfaced before approval in human output.

message: Missing tests for contract.reviewers config rejection paths.
evidence: validateContractReviewers adds blank, unknown, and duplicate-name errors, but no tests reference invalid contract.reviewers entries.

message: Missing test for multiple configured contract reviewers.
evidence: The only successful panel test configures a single reviewer, while the contract requires fan-out across multiple reviewers and all lenses.

message: Human-readable findings output is not tested for configured contract reviewers.
evidence: The finding-emission test uses --json only, but the contract requires findings in both human-readable and JSON output.

message: contractReviewersConfigured silently treats readConfig failures as contract review being disabled, so nextCommandsForRun advertises pactum contract approve when config loading should fail. ContractApprove does not load config, so an invalid configured contract.reviewers entry can be bypassed through approval instead of surfacing the review/config failure.
evidence: resolve.go returns false on readConfig error and then falls through to approve; contract.go ContractApprove loads contract context and writes approval without reading config.

message: Contract review duplicates the existing review-loop fan-out machinery instead of sharing it.
evidence: groupContractReviewerLensTasks, runContractReviewFanOut, runContractReviewerLensGroup, and launchContractReviewerLensTasks mirror the existing review_loop.go grouping/staggering/launch code.

message: SECURITY.md omits `pactum contract review` from the agent-launching command list, even though the new implementation launches reviewer subprocesses for configured contract reviewers. The read-only stages sentence also omits contract review, so the safety model no longer covers every unsandboxed agent-running command.
evidence: SECURITY.md lines 11-18 list agent-launching commands without `pactum contract review`; internal/app/contract_review.go lines 154-204 and 526-555 show the command resolves reviewers and runs lens attempts via agent subprocesses.

message: docs/agents.md says registry-name references are limited to `--agent`, `--reviewer`, and `review.panel`, but this change adds `contract.reviewers` as another registry-backed config reference. The canonical agent/config docs are stale and do not tell users how to configure the new contract review panel.
evidence: docs/agents.md lines 29-31 enumerate the registry references and omit `contract.reviewers`; internal/app/config.go lines 63-68 and 275-296 add and validate `contract.reviewers` as registry names.
